title: Ramayana Trailer Release Date Speculated for November 8
description: The highly anticipated Ramayana trailer, starring Ranbir Kapoor, is rumored to release on November 8, generating significant buzz among fans.

sources.title: 5 Things To Expect From Ranbir Kapoor-Led Ramayana's Grand Trailer
'Ramayana' stars Ranbir Kapoor and Yash ARRIVE at San Diego Comic-Con 2026 for grand trailer launch - PIC
Ramayana trailer buzz grows as November 8 release hinted

sections.heading: Ramayana Trailer Release Speculation
paragraphs: The Ramayana trailer, featuring Ranbir Kapoor in the lead role, has been a hot topic among fans and media. According to MSN, the buzz around the trailer's release is growing as November 8 is hinted as the potential release date.

sections.heading: Ramayana's Presence at San Diego Comic-Con
paragraphs: Adding to the excitement, The Times of India reports that Ranbir Kapoor and Yash arrived at San Diego Comic-Con 2026 for the grand trailer launch, indicating the significance of the event.

sections.heading: Expectations from the Trailer
paragraphs: NDTV outlines five things to expect from the Ramayana trailer, building anticipation for the visual spectacle that the film promises to deliver.

faq.q: What is the speculated release date for the Ramayana trailer?
faq.a: MSN suggests that the Ramayana trailer might be released on November 8.

faq.q: Why is the Ramayana trailer launch a significant event?
faq.a: The Times of India reports that the lead actors, Ranbir Kapoor and Yash, attended San Diego Comic-Con 2026 for the trailer launch, highlighting its importance.

review.notes: rules: all rules checks passed | reviewer(qwen-max): The article is well-structured and properly attributes its claims to the sources provided. The only minor issue is that the speculation about the release date, while attributed, is not a confirmed fact, which slightly impacts the factuality score. However, this is a common practice in entertainment news and the article does a good job of indicating that it is a rumor. The tone is neutral and there are no decency issues.
